WritingPersonas.title: My Personas
WritingPersonas.intro: Save up to three narrator voices you can reuse when creating a story. Most authors only need one custom voice, but this shelf is here when a voice is worth keeping.

signedOut.title: Sign in to manage your personas
signedOut.description: Your writing personas belong to your author account.

guide.title: What does a writing persona change?
guide.description: A persona is the narrator's mask: it changes who speaks, how fast the sentences move, which words feel natural, how much dialogue appears, and which details the story notices first.
guide.link: Read the Mythoria guide to literary personas

create.title: Create a reusable persona
create.description: Give this voice a name so you can pick it again from the story creation flow.

edit.title: Edit persona
edit.description: Tune this saved voice. Future stories will use the updated settings when you select it.

empty.title: No saved personas yet
empty.description: Create one here, or make a one-book custom voice directly in Step 4 when the story needs it.

errors.loadFailed: Could not load your writing personas. Please try again.
errors.saveFailed: Could not save this writing persona. Please try again.
errors.deleteFailed: Could not delete this writing persona. Please try again.

name.label: Persona name
name.help: Only you see this name. Use something you will recognize later.

pov.label: Point of view
pov.help: Choose who tells the story. This changes how close the reader feels to the action and characters.

tone.label: Tone
tone.help: Sets the emotional color of the narrator, from grounded and earnest to playful and funny.

formality.label: Formality
formality.help: Controls whether the voice feels like a close friend, a polished narrator, or a more formal chronicler.

rhythm.label: Rhythm
rhythm.help: Shapes sentence pace and momentum, from reflective paragraphs to quick, energetic beats.

vocabulary.label: Vocabulary
vocabulary.help: Adjusts word complexity so the story can feel plainspoken, rich, poetic, or more advanced.

fictionality.label: Fictionality
fictionality.help: Tells the engine how strictly to stay grounded versus how freely it may heighten events.

dialogueDensity.label: Dialogue Density
dialogueDensity.help: Changes how often characters speak. More dialogue can make the story feel warmer and faster.

sensoriality.label: Sensoriality
sensoriality.help: Controls how much the prose notices sights, sounds, textures, smells, and atmosphere.

subtextIrony.label: Subtext & Irony
subtextIrony.help: Adds hidden meaning, wit, or contrast between what is said and what the story quietly suggests.

techniques.label: Techniques
techniques.help: Optional storytelling tools. Leave them empty when you want a clean, simple narrator.

techniqueHelp.free-indirect-discourse: Blends narration with a character's thoughts.
techniqueHelp.unreliable-narrator: Lets the narrator be partial, mistaken, or playful with truth.
techniqueHelp.4th-wall-break: Allows brief direct winks to the reader.
techniqueHelp.show-dont-tell: Pushes emotion through action and detail.
techniqueHelp.scene-anchoring: Keeps each scene grounded in place and movement.
techniqueHelp.lyrical-refrains: Repeats poetic phrases for rhythm and memory.
techniqueHelp.oral-cadence: Makes the prose feel like a tale told aloud.

specialRequirements.label: Special requirements
specialRequirements.placeholder: e.g., Keep chapters gentle, avoid sarcasm, and make the narrator sound like a warm older cousin.
specialRequirements.help: Add short writing tips for this voice. Do not include private information you do not want used in the story.

story.createOneBook: Create a custom voice for this book
story.customDescription: Tune the narrator for this story only. No name needed, no shelf space used.
story.modalDescription: These settings guide the writing engine for this draft. They shape the narrator's mask, not the facts of your story.
